meta.title: Sejong Yang
meta.description: Sejong Yang's homepage: learner, developer, and researcher.

blog.title: A personal blog with notes, side thoughts, and unnecessary posts
blog.intro: I use the blog for loose notes, build logs, small retrospectives, and whatever else felt worth writing down that day.

lgai_current.desc: I am currently working as an AI Scientist at LG AI Research (EXAONE Lab), contributing to EXAONE 4.5 — a hybrid VLM that unifies reasoning and non-reasoning capabilities in a single vision-language model — and applying it to vertical domain projects across finance, automotive, life science, and defense.

yonsei_phd.desc: I received my Ph.D. in Computer Science from Yonsei University (Sep 2019 – Feb 2026), supervised by Prof. Seon Joo Kim. Thesis: Multi-Prior Alignment for Efficient Generative Vision Tasks. My research focused on face modeling with generative models (VAE, GAN, Normalizing Flows, Diffusion Models), 3D models (3DMM, Neural Renderer, NeRF), and multi-modal representation (visual-audio, visual-text).

lgai_intern.desc: I spent the winter of 2025 as an AI Scientist Intern at LG AI Research (EXAONE Lab) in Seoul, just before completing my Ph.D., contributing to K-EXAONE — Korea's national sovereign AI foundation model — and EXAONE 4.5, a hybrid VLM that unifies reasoning and non-reasoning in a single vision-language model. Mentored by Soyeon Kim.

adobe.desc: I was a research scientist intern at Adobe Research in San Jose, California, in summer 2023, working on arbitrary talking head generation across humans, animals, cartoons, and pareidolia faces with Stable Diffusion. I was fortunate to work with Yang Zhou, Seoung Wug Oh, Cameron Smith, and Deepali Aneja. Check the projects tab for more information.

naver.desc: I interned at NAVER in summer 2020 and participated in the machine learning challenge AI Rush.

yonsei_undergrad_intern.desc: As an undergraduate research intern in Prof. Seon Joo Kim's lab, I assisted with several color-related research projects: an auto white balance (AWB) display project, outdoor illumination observation with a hyperspectral camera, and data collection and refinement for color constancy tasks. I also worked on automatically finding baseball game highlights using video summarization methods.

blammedia.desc: I joined the startup Blammedia as CTO in 2019. We received a preliminary startup package investment worth KRW 30M from the Korea Institute of Startup and Entrepreneurship Development, and I learned the market value of technology and product through meetings with venture capitalists.

uppsala.desc: I spent a spring semester in 2018 as an exchange student at Uppsala University. Studying computer science in a different culture was a great experience.

ewoosoft.desc: In 2017, I worked at Ewoosoft as a developer on EzMobile and Ez3D-i. I learned how to use Xamarin.Forms, Qt5, and Flask for enterprise products, along with collaboration and DevOps tools such as Jira, Atlassian, Crucible and Fisheye, GitHub, and Jenkins in a waterfall development environment.

kakao.desc: I worked as a software assistant at Kakao in 2016, developing a component for OpenStack. Through that experience, I learned the basics of networking, virtualization, and cloud computing.

yonsei_beng.desc: I earned my BEng in Computer Science from Yonsei University. It was where I first learned what coding was, and where I completed my degree in the end. Outside of coursework, I was active in several clubs: served as Vice President of PoolC, the engineering college programming club; competed in poomsae events and performed with the Taekwondo demonstration team; and performed Croatian Rhapsody by Maksim Mrvica on a Steinway piano at Baekjunyeon Memorial Hall as a member of Piano In Yonsei.

misc.games: I enjoy buying many kinds of Steam games.
misc.books: I enjoy reading books across many genres.
misc.piano: I enjoy playing the piano across many genres of music.

meta.title: Projects | Sejong Yang
meta.description: Research and engineering projects by Sejong Yang.

portrait.desc:
- Reproduced Deep3dPortrait as a baseline
- Improved expression manipulation and speed with a state-of-the-art 3DMM model
- Funded by Samsung Research

deepfake.desc:
- Reproduced Noiseprint for blind deepfake detection
- Tried multiple classification models including ResNet+RNN, C3D, and I3D
- Funded by NIS

ssn.desc:
- Investigated efficient sampling for large 3D point clouds
- Explored classification, registration, and restoration settings
- Tried unsupervised importance scoring similar to pruning
- Stopped because there was no sufficiently concrete optimization criterion such as a reliable weight norm signal

srpruning.desc:
- Network pruning for super resolution
- Explored lottery ticket behavior in decoders (Korean blog article)
- Evaluated VDSR, MSRResNet, CARN, SRDenseNet, RDN, RRDB, and RCAN
- Compared against SNIP, GraSP, and SynFlow

landmark.desc:
- Image classification with landmark location labels
- Tried multiple models including EfficientNet, ResNet, ResNeXt, and MoCo v2
- Won event prize money for sharing the code

spam.desc:
- Participated in NAVER AI Rush 2020 round 1
- Advanced to the next round with rank 23
- Tried multiple classification models such as ResNet and EfficientNet
- Explored focal loss, label smoothing, and temperature scaling for imbalance
- Compared optimizers and schedulers such as RAdam and CosineLRScheduler

doughsam.desc:
Django-based Yonsei University class schedule management web service
- Crawled class schedules and mileage history from the Yonsei University database
- Built a schedule editor similar to Everytime

jinglebell.desc:
Glove-hand recognition with OpenCV
Jingle Bell plays when the hands are placed over each bell on the screen
